text: Do you make thoughtless remarks or accusations that you later regret?
When others begin to lose their patience, do you remain fairly calm?
Do you browse through train timetables, telephone directories, or dictionaries for pleasure?
If you were asked to make a decision, could your liking or disliking of the person concerned sway you?
Do you intend to have two children (or fewer) in your family, even if your health and income level would allow you to have more?
Do your muscles cramp from time to time, even when there is no logical explanation for it?
Do you prefer to be in a situation where you don't have to take responsibility for making decisions?
Do other people consider your actions unpredictable?
Do you think that more money should be spent on social welfare?
Are you strongly interested in other people?
Is your voice rather monotonous than varied?
Do you usually wait for the other person to start the conversation?
Do you readily join in on other people's conversations?
Would the thought of causing pain to game (birds), small animals, or fish keep you from hunting or fishing?
Are you often impulsive in your behavior?
Do you speak slowly?
Does the need to look after your health worry you?
Does some unexpected event make your muscles tense up?
Are you usually tactful in your demands on subordinates, relatives, or students?
Do you think you are able to give a valuable "snap judgment"?
Do past failures still trouble you?
Do you have periods of heightened activity lasting several days in a row?
Do other people's efforts to tell you what you should do annoy you?
Do you usually find it difficult to admit something frankly and take the blame?
Do you have a rather small circle of close friends rather than a large number of acquaintances you can simply chat with?
Is your life a constant struggle for survival?
Do you often hum or whistle?
Do your friends consider you a warm, responsive person?
Do you prefer giving orders to carrying them out?
Does it give you pleasure to pass on the latest gossip about your acquaintances?
Would you agree to "strict discipline"?
Does the thought of starting some new undertaking worry you greatly?
Do you try to make other people laugh and smile?
Is it easy for you to express your emotions?
Do you refrain from expressing displeasure if someone is late for a meeting?
Are you sometimes considered a person who spoils other people's mood?
Do you think there are people who are clearly unfriendly toward you and are "scheming" against you?
Can you admit that you were wrong just to "keep the peace"?
Is it true that there are only a few people you truly love?
Is it true that you are rarely happy unless there is a special reason for it?
When in company, do you actively socialize with your friends?
Do you take reasonable precautions to avoid accidents?
Do you get nervous at the thought of having to speak in front of an audience?
If you saw an item in a shop with a clearly mistaken lower price on it, would you try to buy it at that price?
Do you often feel that people are looking at you or talking about you behind your back?
Is it true that you are forever getting into trouble?
Is there something you are particularly afraid of or dislike?
Is it true that you prefer to be a spectator rather than a participant in competitions?
Is it easy for you to take an unbiased view of what has happened?
Do you have clear, settled standards of polite behavior toward the members of your family?
Can you be the "life of the party" at gatherings?
Would you "buy things on credit" in the hope that you would be able to find the means to pay it off?
Does your reaction come only after something unexpected has happened, such as an accident or another unpleasant incident?
Is it true that you take a group's interests into account more than your own personal interests?
When you listen to a lecturer, do you sometimes get the thought that they are addressing you exclusively?
Do extraneous noises rarely stop you from concentrating?
Are you always "up to date" with what is happening day to day?
Are you able to confidently plan something and work toward carrying it out six months in advance?
Do you think that the modern system of "prison without bars" is doomed to fail?
Do you tend to be carefree and not worry about anything?
Do you ever get the feeling that you are dreaming, when everything in life seems unreal?
Do you quickly recover from the effect of bad news?
When you criticize someone, do you try at the same time to encourage them?
Are you usually considered a cold person?
Do you think your opinions are not important enough to share with other people?
Are you so self-assured that it sometimes irritates others?
Do you "keep an eye on" things that you have lent to your friends?
Does an activity you have chosen yourself give you pleasure?
Does emotional music have a strong effect on you?
Is it true that you completely condemn a person simply because they are your rival or opponent on some matter in your relationship with them?
Do you often "sit and brood" about death, illness, pain, and sorrow?
Does the thought of losing your sense of self-worth worry you?
Is it true that you are constantly collecting things that "might come in handy later"?
Would you criticize shortcomings and point out to a person the bad traits of their character or the poor work they have done?
Do you openly express your admiration for beautiful things?
Does it happen that you give away things that, strictly speaking, do not belong to you?
Do you greet people in an emotional way?
Do you often dwell on past failures?
Are you sometimes considered a domineering person in your actions and views?
Do you take criticism easily and without taking offense?
Does "complete silence" usually not bother you when you want to rest?
Are you inclined to be jealous?
Is it true that you tend to put things off until later, and then you find that it is already too late?
Is it true that you prefer to act in accordance with the wishes of others rather than striving to do everything your own way?
Is it easy for you to get started on some task?
Do you have a habit of biting your nails or the end of a pencil?
Does it happen that, to create some effect, you exaggerate the degree to which you express your emotions?
If your country invaded another country, would you sympathize with those of its citizens who refused military service on political or religious grounds?
Do you have sensitive spots that cause you a painful reaction when touched upon?
Is it true that you have a small range of interests and activities that you pursue by your own choice?
Does it happen that a thought takes hold of your mind for several days?
Do you eat slowly?
Can you have a stabilizing effect when others begin to panic?
Would you stop and try to find out whether a person needs help, even if they haven't directly asked for it?
Do you tend to think that your school, college, club, team, etc. are better than others?
Do you pay your debts and keep your promises whenever possible?
Do you sleep well?
Would you use corporal punishment on a 10-year-old child if they refused to obey you?
Do you prefer to take a passive role in any group or organization you belong to?
Is there logic and a scientific approach in your way of thinking?
Is it true that today's youth have more opportunities than the youth of the previous generation?
Does it happen that you throw away your things and later find that you need them?
Would you readily give up a chosen course of action if it caused you even a few difficulties?
Is it true that only certain specific topics deeply move you?
Is it true that you rarely question other people's actions?
Is it true that you sometimes ask yourself whether anyone really cares about you?
Do you decline responsibility for something because of your doubts that you might not be able to handle it?
Do you feel that you sometimes have to repeat some interesting piece of news or rumor several times?
Do you tend to exaggerate a justified grievance?
Is it true that your facial expression is changeable rather than constant?
Do you usually need confirmation or rejection of an opinion you have once expressed?
Do you openly and sincerely admire the beauty of other people?
Do you find it difficult to consider the subject of suicide?
Do you consider yourself energetic in your attitude toward life?
Can a difference of opinion affect your overall relationship with another person?
Is it true that your minor setbacks rarely trouble you?
Do you sometimes feel that you talk too much?
Do you smile often?
Are you a person who is easy to please?
When you meet open resistance, do you still try to do something your own way?
If the distance is not too great, do you prefer to take transport rather than walk?
Does the sound of the wind or of a "settling house" ever bother you?
Is your opinion shaped by viewing things from the perspective of your life experience, profession, or education?
Do you often make tactless mistakes?
Are you suspicious of people who ask to borrow money from you?
Do your personal interests influence your decisions?
Can you become delighted by some "simple little thing"?
Do you often take action even though your own common sense points to the contrary?
Are you in favor of dividing people by skin color and class?
Do you have habits such as stroking your hair, nose, ears, etc.?
Can you quickly adapt to and make use of new conditions and situations, even if they may turn out to be difficult?
Can certain sounds cause you extremely unpleasant sensations, such as "setting your teeth on edge"?
Can you understand another person's point of view if you want to?
Do you go to bed when you feel like it rather than by the clock?
Do you lose patience over the "petty flaws" of other people?
Do children irritate you?
Are you less talkative than your acquaintances?
Do you usually carry out tasks quickly and systematically?
Would you help a fellow traveler rather than leave it to the officials?
When you vote, do you vote for the same party instead of studying the candidates and their election programs?
Do you often dwell on your past illnesses and painful experiences?
Do you feel quite uneasy when there is a mess around you?
Do you usually criticize a film or play you are watching, or a book you are reading?
When telling an amusing story, can you easily imitate the manners or speech of those involved in it?
In areas where you are not an expert, is your opinion important enough to express to other people?
Are you inclined to tidy up in someone else's home if it is messy?
Is it easy for you to accept defeat without feeling frustrated?
Do you often feel depressed?
Do you feel awkward in the company of children?
Do you get upset when you can't do something, instead of finding a substitute for that activity?
Are you sometimes unable to fully grasp the essence of things?
Do you rarely express your displeasure?
Do you work in "bursts," being relatively passive and then overly active for a day or two?
Does a certain number of unfinished current tasks bother you?
Do people enjoy being in your company?
Can you refrain from interfering when someone else is finishing a crossword puzzle?
Do you take into account the better sides of most people and only rarely speak of them disrespectfully?
Do you laugh or smile readily?
Are your voice and manner definite and expressive?
Do you show your short temper only toward close friends, if you show it at all?
Are your interests and areas of knowledge so important to you that they leave you little time for anything else?
Would you like to start a "new venture" near the place where you live?
Would you take the necessary action to kill an animal in order to relieve it of pain?
Is it easy for you to relax?
Do you have few regrets about past hardships and failures?
Do gloomy forebodings or the thought of fear cause you a physical reaction?
Can you rely on what your common sense tells you in an emotional situation you have found yourself in?
Could someone else consider you truly active?
Do you find it difficult to get down to a task that needs to be done?
Are you opposed to the "probation system" for criminals?
Do you spend a lot of time worrying over trifles?
During an argument, do you find it hard to understand why the other person cannot accept your point of view and thus agree with you?
Do you cope well enough with the problems of everyday life?
Are you usually truthful with those around you?
Do you prefer to wait for events to unfold rather than start acting yourself?
Do you spend money as easily as you earn it?
Are you able to take a "reasonable risk" without much anxiety?
If you were in a minor car accident, would you take the trouble to see that the damage you caused was made good?
Do other people push you around?
Do you show leniency toward your friends in cases where you would treat others more strictly?
Do you often dwell on your own inferiority?
Does it happen that people criticize you in conversation with other people?
Do displays of warmth at a greeting, such as a kiss, a firm hug, or a pat on the back, embarrass you if they take place in public?
Do you often have to refrain from doing what you would like to do, out of consideration for other people's wishes?
Are you often convinced that your view on a subject is correct, even if you are not an expert on the matter?
Do you often find yourself in situations where you have to "handle everything" at once ("work on several fronts")?
Does it seem to you that your acquaintances have a higher opinion of your abilities than you do yourself?
Does the thought of death, or even a reminder of it, horrify you?
After settling a disputed matter, do you continue to feel dissatisfied for some time?
Is there friendliness in your voice, attitude, and facial expression?
Does life seem unreal and rather hazy to you?
Do you often get upset over the fate of war victims and political refugees?
Do "mere acquaintances" turn to you for help or advice with their personal problems?
If you lose something, does the thought occur to you that "someone must have stolen it or put it in the wrong place"?
If you thought that someone regarded you and your actions with suspicion, would you take the matter up with them rather than leave them to figure it all out for themselves?
Do you feel that your age works against you (that you are too young or too old)?
Do you have bouts of sadness and depression for no reason at all?
Do you often grumble about the living conditions you have to deal with?
Do you tend to hide your feelings?
Do you think you have many close friends?
